summary: Iteration 4 backend tests: 20/22 PASSED, 2 FAILED — both failures caused by a single backend route-ordering bug that completely breaks GET /api/users/search. All other new features (profile update + validation, /users/me/profile, /users/{id}, /countries, /platforms-list, POST /challenges with self/zero-stake/missing-opponent/missing-game/insufficient-balance guards, GET /challenges/incoming, private-tournament invite-only join enforcement, hiding private tournaments from public listing, last_active_at fire-and-forget update) work correctly. All 42 previous-iteration tests still pass.

critical.issue: Route is completely broken — returns 404 'User not found' for every query (verified via curl as admin and via authenticated test sessions). Root cause: in /app/backend/server.py the dynamic route @api_router.get('/users/{user_id}') is declared at line 454, BEFORE the static route @api_router.get('/users/search') at line 464. FastAPI matches routes in declaration order, so any GET /users/search request is captured by /users/{user_id} with user_id='search', which fails ObjectId parsing and returns 404. Fix: move the /users/search route definition (and any other static /users/* routes) ABOVE the /users/{user_id} route. Also note /users/me/profile works only because it is declared at line 449 BEFORE /users/{user_id}.

minor.issue: country is stored exactly as the client sends it (no uppercase normalization). However GET /api/users/search uppercases its country filter (`country.upper()`), so a user who set country='au' will never match a search for country='AU'. Recommend uppercasing country on write (or lowercasing on both sides) to keep storage consistent with the search filter.

Stake is deducted before tournament insert, but if the insert fails the deduction is not rolled back. Also there is no DB transaction wrapping the deduction + tournament insert + participant insert. Low risk in practice but worth flagging.

Returns the full _build_public_profile payload including wallet_balance for ANY authenticated viewer. Wallet balance arguably should not be exposed on a public profile — review whether to redact it for non-self lookups.

action_items: FIX (critical): reorder routes in /app/backend/server.py so that all static /users/* routes (/users/profile, /users/me/profile, /users/search) are declared BEFORE the dynamic /users/{user_id} route. Currently /users/search is shadowed and returns 404 for every request.
Normalize country to uppercase on PUT /api/users/profile to align with the uppercase comparison in /api/users/search.
Consider not returning wallet_balance from GET /api/users/{user_id} (public profile of another user).
Consider refactoring /api/users/search to a POST or to fix route ordering — and add an integration test that asserts /api/users/search returns 200 with a list when called by any authenticated user.
critical_code_review_comments: Route-ordering bug (above) completely disables user search — this is the only blocker.

server.py is now ~1370 lines and continues to grow (profiles, challenges, search added). Strongly recommend splitting into routers/ modules (auth, users, tournaments, evidence, latency, wallet, challenges) before further iterations.

last_active_at update uses asyncio.create_task on a coroutine result inside a sync try/except; the task is fire-and-forget and never awaited or logged, so update failures will silently disappear. Consider logging exceptions from the task with task.add_done_callback.

/api/users/search runs an unbounded $regex on username AND bio with user-supplied q — case-insensitive regex on free text is a performance and ReDoS risk on a growing user collection. Sanitize/escape q (re.escape) and add an index, or switch to text index.

_build_public_profile is called inside a per-user for-loop in search → N additional DB queries per result for preferred_games. For 50 results this is up to 50*N queries. Batch fetch games with $in once.
PUT /api/users/profile validates preferred_game_ids one-by-one in a loop with individual find_one queries. Use one find with {_id: {$in: [...]}} and compare set sizes.

POST /api/challenges: tournament_doc lacks 'updated_at' and the participants doc reuses 'pending' as result_status but server.py elsewhere has no consistent enum — consider documenting status state machine.
GET /api/users/{user_id} returns wallet_balance to any authenticated viewer; this is real-money-equivalent info even in play-money mode.

Search filter 'online_only' uses ISO-string comparison to a threshold. This works because ISO 8601 with fixed offsets sorts lexicographically, but only if every record uses the same offset format. last_active_at is written with datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat() (always +00:00), so OK today — but document this invariant.
test_credentials: Admin admin@esportsbet.com / admin123 verified working. Iteration-4 tests register fresh TEST_ users per scenario and seed wallets via direct pymongo update.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-prefixed users + TEST_ games per scenario. last_active_at is forced via pymongo for the stale-user search assertion. No production data modified.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After the route-ordering fix in server.py, simply re-run `python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter4_profiles_challenges.py -v` — the two failing tests (TestUserSearch::test_search_excludes_self_and_filters and TestUserSearch::test_search_online_only) will pass. All 22 iter4 tests + 42 legacy tests should then be green. The legacy suite (/app/backend/tests/backend_test.py + test_new_features.py) is unaffected by this iteration and was re-verified green (42/42).
rca of the issue: BUG: GET /api/users/search returns 404 'User not found' for every request. REPRODUCTION: 1) Login as any user. 2) curl -b cookies.txt 'https://esports-bet-3.preview.emergentagent.com/api/users/search?country=AU' → 404 {'detail':'User not found'}. ROOT CAUSE: In /app/backend/server.py the dynamic route @api_router.get('/users/{user_id}', line 454) is registered before the static route @api_router.get('/users/search', line 464). FastAPI/Starlette matches routes in registration order, so /users/search is routed into get_user_profile(user_id='search'), which fails ObjectId parsing and returns 404. MITIGATION: Move the /users/search definition (and any future static /users/* endpoints) above the @api_router.get('/users/{user_id}') definition. /users/me/profile is unaffected because it is declared BEFORE /users/{user_id}.
